bead.title: pre-claim intake: decode readiness_checks[].verdict as JSON bool, string, or absent
bead.description:
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder rejects the readiness payload when a readiness_checks entry's `verdict` is a JSON bool, emitting `decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string`. Under warn-only policy this skips every bead, idling a full fizeau queue (23 ready beads, 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC). The identical error fingerprint (15155a1c...) across distinct bead_ids confirms a producer/consumer schema mismatch, not a bead defect.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go` — `preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict` (struct at :75) bound `verdict` to a plain string, so `decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResultWithMode` (:688) and `decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult` (:681) fail when the readiness producer emits `verdict:true`. A verdict may legitimately be a JSON bool, a JSON string (e.g. "fail"/"pass"/"ready"), or be omitted.

PROPOSED FIX
Give the field a dedicated `readinessVerdict` type (:91) with a custom UnmarshalJSON that accepts a JSON bool (coerced to a documented canonical lowercased string), a JSON string (trimmed), and JSON null/absent (empty), and rejects any other JSON kind with a descriptive error. Downstream comparisons in `failedReadinessReasons` (:823) and `readinessCheckEvidence` (:848) operate on the canonical lowercased string.

NON-SCOPE
Does NOT add the shared JSON schema or CI drift gate (covered by the schema-lock child). Does NOT change the worker same-fingerprint escalation (escalation child), the rolling claim-success-rate signal (claim-rate child), or README docs (docs child). Preserves the FEAT-010 / ADR-023 readiness vocabulary and cross-refs ddx-57c40485, ddx-8f2e0ebf, ddx-9714eaac.
bead.acceptance:
1. `readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON` in `cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go` accepts a JSON bool, a JSON string, and JSON null/absent, coercing each to a documented canonical lowercased string, and returns a descriptive error for any other JSON kind. Asserted by `TestReadinessVerdict_AcceptsBoolStringOrNull` (add if absent).
2. `decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult` decodes a payload whose `readiness_checks[].verdict` is `true`/`false` without the `cannot unmarshal bool` error and yields the expected failed-reason set. Asserted by `TestReadinessClassification_DecodesReadinessSchema` and the `TestPreClaimReadiness_Normalizes*` family in `cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification_test.go`.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes
